items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Assuming each of Kessler, Mamukelashvili & Grimes start at the lowest salary number possible to hit their reported totals, the Lakers are about $209K over the cap.

That's why something else has to be coming to fit all of these deals in.

Reaves and Sexton signings will come after cap space is used.

items.title: My quick thoughts:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
My quick thoughts:

-Lakers need a real PF
-Team is still short on perimeter defense
-Could still use more shooting

Maybe trades come to round things out, but there are real holes for the Lakers right now, despite adding a bunch of guys today.

items.title: Lakers order of operations:
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Lakers order of operations:

$52+ million in cap space: 
🏀Sign and trade for Walker Kessler (~$30.2 million)
🏀Sign Quentin Grimes (~$14 million)
🏀Sign Sandro Mamukelashvili (~$12.1 million)

$9.4 million room mid-level:
🏀Sign Collin Sexton

$14.1 million under the first apron.

items.title: Strong haul by the Jazz, capitalizing on a weak center market. Danny Ainge strikes again. And Kessler with a big win for a RFA.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Strong haul by the Jazz, capitalizing on a weak center market. Danny Ainge strikes again. And Kessler with a big win for a RFA.

Pacers were wise to get their guy — Zubac — at the trade deadline to fill that void. And he’s under contract for the next two years at ~$21M per.

items.title: Piecing together all of the Los Angeles Lakers deals:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Piecing together all of the Los Angeles Lakers deals:

Walker Kessler, Quentin Grimes and Sandro Mamukelashvili will all go into the Lakers cap space.

Collin Sexton will come in via the Room Exception.

LA probably needs to get off a contract by my count but this is the Lakers offseason wrapped up.
